sn1.14:1.1:
v.26 "The khattiya is the best of bipeds,
The ox, the best of quadrupeds;
A maiden is the best of wives,
The first born, the best of sons."[n.23] From this point on, wherever the text does not specify the identity of the speakers, it is implied that the first verse is spoken by a devata and the reply by the Buddha.
sn1.14:2.1:
v.27"The Buddha is the best of bipeds,
A steed, the best of quadrupeds;
An obedient woman is the best of wives,
A dutiful boy, the best of sons." sn.i.7
